mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive 20d returns occur in >65% of high-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate above 5.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60d returns in >60% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.5%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC < 0.02 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, which drives corporate earnings expectations higher and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate a positive Rank IC > 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in <55% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints and weakening economic momentum, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles are followed by positive 5d returns in >65% of cases, or the regime-conditional signal shows no statistical edge over a baseline model.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0%, the cost of capital becomes sufficiently high to suppress corporate investment and equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20d returns are positive in >60% of observations when FEDFUNDS > 4.0%, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a stable or declining unemployment rate signals a strengthening labor market that supports consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction signal yields a negative Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window, or positive 60d returns occur in <50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth rates reflect expanding economic activity and corporate revenues, which directly translate into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 shows no significant correlation with 20d forward returns (Rank IC < 0.02) over any 2-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in <55% of high-GDP-growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation during a period of stable interest rates erodes real returns and prompts expectations of future tightening, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Rising CPIAUCSL during stable-rate periods is followed by positive 5d returns in >65% of cases, or the interaction signal fails to achieve a Rank IC > 0.03 in out-of-sample testing.
